div.head: The Counselor of the British Embassy (Barclay) to the Assistant Secretary of State (Phillips)
div.p:
My Dear Mr. Phillips: On December 10th Mr. ChiltonHenry G. Chilton, Secretary of the British Embassy. handed to Mr. JonesRussell C. Jones, of the Office of the Assistant Secretary of State. a copy of a telegram from His Majesty’s Representative at Constantinople to the Foreign Office dated December 5th embodying a message from Dr. Gates to the Department of State.Not printed. I have now received a further telegram from Constantinople through the Foreign Office stating that Dr. Gates asks that the following may be transmitted to the State Department:
“When will the Belief Expedition fitting out for Turkey sail? Greatest need of haste.
“Conditions here are becoming desperate. Turkish currency much depreciated. Expedition should bring gold and as much coal as possible.”
